name: invest-03-flaky-test-rootcause
initialPrompt: Our CI pipeline is failing 20-30% of the time on a non-deterministic test suite failure — different tests fail each run. This is killing developer confidence and blocking releases. I need a plan to: systematically identify which tests are flaky and why (timing, shared state, or external dependencies), fix the root causes, and set up a flakiness tracking mechanism so we stop merging new flaky tests going forward.

cannedAuthorResponse.title: Flaky test root-cause investigation and remediation

blocks.text: Reduce CI failure rate from ~25% to < 1% by eliminating flaky tests
successCriteria: no new flaky tests merged for 30 days

blocks.title: Collect CI failure logs for last 30 runs and tag which tests failed each time
acceptance: spreadsheet of test name vs run outcome
top-10 most-flaky tests identified by failure frequency

blocks.title: Classify each flaky test by root cause: timing, shared state, or network
acceptance: each of top-10 tests has a documented hypothesis

blocks.title: Fix shared-state flakes by adding proper test isolation (beforeEach teardown)
acceptance: each shared-state flake passes 50 consecutive local runs
no global mutable singletons left in test helpers

blocks.question: How do we fix timing-dependent tests — fixed waits, retry polling, or deterministic fakes?

blocks.description: Tests appear fixed locally but still flake in CI due to different concurrency
blocks.mitigation: Run fixed tests 100 times in CI matrix before removing quarantine label

blocks.question: Are any flaky tests hitting real external services (payment sandbox, email) that should be mocked?

cannedForcedReviseResponse.title: Flaky test root-cause investigation and remediation

blocks.text: Reduce CI failure rate from ~25% to < 1% by eliminating flaky tests
successCriteria: no new flaky tests merged for 30 days
developer confidence survey score improves by 2 points

blocks.title: Export CI run outcomes to a structured dataset for the last 60 runs
acceptance: JSON dataset with test name, run id, outcome, duration

blocks.title: Collect CI failure logs and tag which tests failed each time
acceptance: spreadsheet of test name vs run outcome
top-10 most-flaky tests identified by failure frequency

blocks.title: Classify each flaky test by root cause: timing, shared state, or network
acceptance: each of top-10 tests has a documented hypothesis

blocks.title: Fix shared-state flakes by adding proper test isolation (beforeEach teardown)
acceptance: each shared-state flake passes 50 consecutive local runs
no global mutable singletons left in test helpers

blocks.question: How do we fix timing-dependent tests — fixed waits, retry polling, or deterministic fakes?
blocks.rationale: Eliminates real timing dependency; makes tests fast and predictable.

blocks.title: Add pre-merge flakiness gate: block PR if test has failed > 2% in last 100 runs
acceptance: CI step added that queries flakiness DB
PR blocked automatically when threshold breached

blocks.description: Tests appear fixed locally but still flake in CI due to different concurrency
blocks.mitigation: Run fixed tests 100 times in CI matrix before removing quarantine label

blocks.question: Are any flaky tests hitting real external services (payment sandbox, email) that should be mocked?

idChangedButCorresponding.note: Same intent (whether flaky tests hit real external services and need mocking) but id renamed during revise. AC-13 re-anchoring maps a comment on oq-external-services to oq-mock-boundary.

decoy.note: Genuinely new block. Shares surface tokens 'flakiness' and 'CI' with task-quarantine-log, making it a trap. A comment on task-quarantine-log must stay there, not mis-attach to task-flakiness-gate.

notes: Renumbering pressure: task-ci-metrics-export inserted near top shifting positional alignment; task-flakiness-gate added as new block; oq-external-services renamed to oq-mock-boundary. Naive positional scheme would misalign task-quarantine-log. expectedPreservedIds excludes renamed open-question and two new tasks.
